varieties.name: Genovese Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: Genoa, Italy; traditional Italian variety, 100+ years old
varieties.traits: Large, dark green, slightly cupped leaves 7-10cm long. Strong, pungent aroma with sweet anise notes. Grows 45-60cm tall with bushy habit.
varieties.yield_notes: High - produces 20+ harvests per plant with regular cutting. Can yield 6-8 bunches per square meter.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Bunnings, nurseries throughout Brisbane/Queensland. Seed available from The Seed Collection, Diggers Club, Eden Seeds.

varieties.name: Napoletano Basil (Lettuce Leaf) 🏆
varieties.origin: Naples, Italy; heirloom from Campania region, first offered by Franchi Sementi
varieties.traits: Massive crinkled leaves 10-15cm long, light green with blistered texture. Slow to bolt. Grows 50-60cm tall.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high due to large leaf size. Single leaves can wrap sandwiches or be used as herb wraps.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers - The Seed Collection, select Brisbane nurseries, Diggers Club, online from Eden Seeds.

varieties.name: Sweet Basil (Ocimum basilicum)
varieties.origin: Native to tropical regions of Central Africa to Southeast Asia, cultivated 5000+ years
varieties.traits: Bright green oval leaves 5-8cm, tender, aromatic. Standard variety. Grows 30-45cm tall.
varieties.yield_notes: High with regular harvesting. Produces continuously when kept from flowering.
varieties.availability: Universally available - all Brisbane nurseries, Bunnings, supermarkets (living herbs), seed from all major suppliers.

varieties.name: Mammoth Basil
varieties.origin: Italian selection, bred for large leaf size
varieties.traits: Extra-large leaves up to 12cm, smooth, bright green. Vigorous growth to 60cm tall.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high due to large leaf size. Excellent for pesto production.
varieties.availability: Select Brisbane nurseries, The Seed Collection, online seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Thai Basil (Siam Queen)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia (Thailand/Vietnam), Siam Queen developed 1997, AAS Award Winner
varieties.traits: Dark green serrated leaves, purple stems and flowers. Grows 45-60cm tall. Leaves 4cm long, 2cm wide. Late flowering.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high. Siam Queen produces larger leaves than standard Thai basil. Slow bolting extends harvest.
varieties.availability: Common in Brisbane - Asian grocery nurseries, Bunnings, Brisbane nurseries. Seed from The Seed Collection, West Coast Seeds, specialist suppliers.

varieties.name: Lemon Basil (Ocimum × citriodorum)
varieties.origin: Natural hybrid between O. basilicum and O. americanum, Southeast Asian origins
varieties.traits: Light green, narrow leaves with citrus scent. Compact growth 30-45cm. White flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high. Fast-growing in warm conditions.

varieties.name: Lime Basil
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, similar to lemon basil
varieties.traits: Similar to lemon basil but with darker leaves and more compact growth. Small plant 25-40cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Compact size makes it ideal for container growing.
varieties.availability: Select Brisbane nurseries, The Seed Collection, specialty suppliers.

varieties.name: Dark Opal Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: University of Connecticut, developed 1950s by John Scarchuk & Joseph Lent. AAS Award 1962
varieties.traits: Deep purple leaves, sometimes mottled. Lavender flowers. Grows 45-50cm tall. High anthocyanin content.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Slow to bolt. Recovers well after cutting.
varieties.availability: Available Brisbane nurseries (seasonal), Bunnings, The Seed Collection, Eden Seeds, Diggers Club.

varieties.name: Purple Ruffles Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, developed 1984 by Ted Torrey (Burpee). Cross of Green Ruffles × Dark Opal. AAS Award 1987
varieties.traits: Deeply serrated, ruffled dark purple leaves 7-8cm long. Striking ornamental appearance. Grows 45-60cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. More pigmentation than Dark Opal. Resist bolting better than standard purple basil.
varieties.availability: Specialty Brisbane nurseries, The Seed Collection, online seed suppliers, Diggers Club.

varieties.name: Cinnamon Basil (Mexican Basil) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mexico/Mediterranean region, cultivated for centuries. Also called Mexican Spice Basil
varieties.traits: Dark green leaves with purple veins, cinnamon-colored stems. Grows 45-90cm tall. Pink-purple flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Vigorous in hot weather. Downy mildew resistant.

varieties.name: Greek Basil (Spicy Globe)
varieties.origin: Greece/Mediterranean, bred from O. basilicum var. minimum
varieties.traits: Tiny leaves less than 1cm, forms perfect 15-20cm globe. Very compact, dense growth. Looks like miniature hedge.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate but continuous. Perfect for regular snipping. Slow to bolt.
varieties.availability: Common in Brisbane - most nurseries, Bunnings, The Seed Collection. Popular for Brisbane apartment gardens.

varieties.name: Dwarf Basil (Fine Verde)
varieties.origin: European bush basil variety
varieties.traits: Small, narrow leaves, compact plant 20-30cm. Dense, bushy growth.
varieties.yield_notes: Good for size. Continuous harvest with regular picking.
varieties.availability: Select Brisbane nurseries, specialty suppliers, The Seed Collection.

varieties.name: African Blue Basil
varieties.origin: Hybrid: Ocimum kilimandscharicum (Camphor Basil, E. Africa) × O. basilicum 'Dark Opal'. Developed 1980s
varieties.traits: Purple-veined dark green leaves, purple flowers. Grows 90-120cm tall, woody stems. Sterile - propagated by cuttings only. Flowers year-round.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high over time. Never bolts to seed. Continuous flowering attracts bees year-round.
varieties.availability: Basilea Living Herbs Burpengary (local Brisbane), Mudbrick Herb Cottage, specialty Brisbane nurseries. Must buy as plant (no seeds).

varieties.name: Rama Tulsi (Krishna Tulsi) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: India (North Central origin), cultivated 5000+ years. Most common Tulsi in Kerala homes
varieties.traits: Bright green leaves, sometimes with purple tinge. Grows 45-60cm tall. Forms 35cm domes. Aromatic subshrub. Strong camphor and cinnamon scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous year-round in Kerala. Perennial that grows for years. Daily leaf picking for worship.
varieties.availability: Universally available throughout Kerala - every nursery, temple grounds, local plant sellers, agricultural dept. Cultural staple in Kerala gardens and households.

varieties.name: Krishna Tulsi (Shyam Tulsi) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: India, sacred purple variety of holy basil
varieties.traits: Purple-green leaves, darker than Rama Tulsi. Grows 45-60cm tall. More intense coloration.
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous perennial growth in Kerala tropical climate.
varieties.availability: Kerala nurseries, temple plant distributions, agricultural centers, Ayurvedic medicine suppliers.

varieties.name: Vana Tulsi (Wild Tulsi) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: India (Ocimum gratissimum), woody perennial variety
varieties.traits: Large, fuzzy leaves. Grows taller than other Tulsi - up to 90cm. Woody stems. Looks more like mint than typical basil.
varieties.yield_notes: Woody perennial provides long-term harvest in tropical Kerala.

varieties.name: Kapoor Tulsi (Temperate Tulsi) 🌏
varieties.origin: India, variety suited to different regions
varieties.traits: Green leaves with purplish-pink flowers. Bubble-gum scent (distinctive!). Fast-growing.
varieties.yield_notes: High. Fast-growing variety produces abundant foliage.
varieties.availability: Increasing availability in Kerala nurseries, specialty herb suppliers, online seed sources.

varieties.name: Genovese Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: Genoa, Italy; introduced to India through trade
varieties.traits: Large dark green leaves, strong aromatic oils. Classic Italian basil. Grows 45-60cm.
varieties.yield_notes: High with regular harvesting in tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Major Kerala nurseries (Kochi, Trivandrum, Kozhikode), agricultural stores, increasing availability in Thiruvalla area.

varieties.name: Sweet Basil (Ocimum basilicum)
varieties.origin: Native to tropical regions including India
varieties.traits: Bright green oval leaves, standard culinary basil. Grows 30-45cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high in tropical Kerala climate. Can produce continuously.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Kerala agricultural stores, nurseries throughout state, local plant vendors in Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Napoletano Basil (Lettuce Leaf) 🏆
varieties.origin: Naples, Italy
varieties.traits: Massive crinkled leaves 10-15cm. Slow bolting. Light green color.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high due to large leaf size.
varieties.availability: Specialty Kerala nurseries, imported seeds from international suppliers, online seed sources.

varieties.name: Thai Basil (Horapha)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia (Thailand/Vietnam), native to tropical regions
varieties.traits: Dark green serrated leaves, purple stems and flowers. Grows 45-60cm. Anise scent.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high in tropical conditions. Slow bolting.
varieties.availability: Growing availability in Kerala - Kochi nurseries, specialty herb suppliers, agricultural research stations. Seeds from Kerala agricultural department.

varieties.name: Thai Basil (Siam Queen)
varieties.origin: Improved Thai basil cultivar, AAS Winner 1997
varieties.traits: Larger leaves than standard Thai basil. Purple stems, late flowering. Very productive.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent. Improved variety with higher yields.

varieties.name: Lemon Basil
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia/India, hybrid O. basilicum × O. americanum
varieties.traits: Light green narrow leaves with strong citrus scent. Compact 30-45cm growth. White flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: High. Fast-growing in tropical heat.
varieties.availability: Kerala nurseries (especially coastal areas), agricultural stores, local plant vendors.

varieties.name: Cinnamon Basil (Mexican Basil) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mexico/Mediterranean, heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Dark green leaves with purple veins, cinnamon-colored stems. Pink-purple flowers. Grows 45-90cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Very vigorous in extreme heat. Downy mildew resistant.

varieties.name: African Blue Basil
varieties.origin: Hybrid: E. African Camphor Basil × Dark Opal, 1980s
varieties.traits: Purple-veined leaves, purple flowers year-round. Grows 90-150cm tall. Woody perennial. Sterile.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high over time. Never sets seed. Continuous flowering.
varieties.availability: Must buy as plant (no seeds). Specialty Kerala herb nurseries, can be propagated from cuttings locally.

varieties.name: Greek Basil (Spicy Globe)
varieties.origin: Greece/Mediterranean
varieties.traits: Tiny leaves <1cm, perfect 15-20cm globe shape. Very compact. Dense foliage.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate but continuous. Slow to bolt.
varieties.availability: Major Kerala city nurseries, specialty suppliers, seed from international sources.

varieties.name: Dark Opal Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, University of Connecticut, 1950s, AAS Award 1962
varieties.traits: Deep purple leaves, sometimes mottled. Lavender flowers. 45-50cm tall.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Slow to bolt.
varieties.availability: Limited in Kerala - specialty nurseries in major cities, international seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Purple Ruffles Basil 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, Ted Torrey/Burpee, 1984, AAS Award 1987
varieties.traits: Deeply ruffled dark purple leaves. Striking ornamental. 45-60cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. More bolting resistance than other purple basils.
varieties.availability: Rare in Kerala - international seed sources, specialty importers.

varieties.name: Heirloom Varieties Highlighted: 15+ traditional varieties marked with
